description: Full-text query composes with the normal pipeline: the FTS5 MATCH set is filtered by a where-clause and bounded by limit, preserving FTS rank order. The top FTS-rank match (note_1) is tag=personal, so the tag=data filter DROPS it and the result is the next-rank data note (note_4) — this makes the where-clause load-bearing: ignoring it would wrongly return note_1. Pins that text_search is not a bypass (DL-15). The where-clause filters the UNINDEXED `tag` field, so the planner ALSO emits a planner.full_scan(no_index) warning for that branch even while uses_index stays true for the FTS match (review 045/046 finding 2; dynamic-indexes.md:139).

indexes.ddl: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "fts_records_notes_f_alice_0" USING fts5(record_id UNINDEXED, value, tokenize = 'unicode61');

warnings.note: review 045/046 finding 2: text_search is not a bypass. The FTS MATCH serves the search (uses_index stays true), but the where-clause filters the UNINDEXED display field `tag` (f_alice_1 is indexed:false), so that branch still scans records. dynamic-indexes.md:139 requires a planner.full_scan warning for the uncovered predicate.
